name: TAO Post Expansion Runner v1
thesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for a post-expansion partial-profit runner only if the entry itself is broad enough to participate. The edge should come from harvesting the first post-expansion move while leaving a smaller core for persistence when volatility keeps expanding.
invalidation_condition: Abort if post-expansion participation remains too sparse, if expansions mean-revert immediately, or if the runner logic has no opportunity to matter after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A fresh expansion bar shifts the short-term range, and continuation becomes tradable when price stays aligned with fast value on the following bar rather than instantly snapping back.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Even in chop, some expansions lead to a second leg. Taking partial profits quickly while leaving a small runner is a management test on top of a deliberately broad participation trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best structurally different lane because volatility expansions are clearer and the runner component has a realistic chance to matter if persistence appears.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If TAO expansions revert immediately, the runner layer will never matter and the setup becomes a weak one-bar chase.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries engage but the runner is consistently dead money, keep the entry and replace the runner with a full two-target exit.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; expansion and post-expansion alignment are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by using broad expansion thresholds rather than requiring rare breakout stacks

long: Close - Open >= 0.4 * ATR_14 OR Close[1] - Open[1] >= 0.4 * ATR_14
short: Open - Close >= 0.4 * ATR_14 OR Open[1] - Close[1] >= 0.4 * ATR_14
